title: Sports
metaDescription: Discover sports at Rosehill College! Our sports program encourages teamwork, resilience, and school spirit through a wide range of athletic opportunities. Learn about the sports we offer, meet our talented teams, and find out how you can get involved in our dynamic sports community.

blocks.text: Sports Our college is committed to fostering a culture of sportsmanship and integrity, encouraging active participation in a diverse range of sports. We believe in the importance of hard work and honesty, both on and off the field. Our dedicated staff members are passionate about nurturing the holistic development of our students, recognising that sports play a crucial role in creating enriching experiences. Here, hard work and honesty are not just values we uphold—they are achievements we celebrate. College Sport Draws & Results Rugby Fixtures & Results External Information For more information regarding Rosehill College sports, please visit our external sports information page. Here, you will find details on fixtures, results, team rosters, training schedules, and other relevant sports-related matters. Rosehill College Sports Page Follow Us Rosehill College Sports Contact Sports DirectorSaua Leaupepetele s.leaupepetele@rosehillcollege.school.nz Sports Coordinator sports@rosehillcollege.school.nz Sports Office Phone  09 295 0661 Ext 935 Code of Conduct All students participating in Sport Department activities must: Be reliable by attending all meetings, practices and games. Wear the correct uniform, where the uniform is borrowed from the sports office it is to be maintained in a neat and tidy condition and returned at the end of the playing season. Abide by the “Student Athlete Policy” of Rosehill College – includes attendance, class performance and behaviour. Fair Play Rules Treat opponents with respect. Play hard but within the rules. Exercise self control at all time (towards opponents, officials and spectators). Respect and accept the decisions of officials without gesture or argument. Always do your best but accept victory or defeat with dignity. Show that it is a privilege to represent your school. Recognise and applaud honestly the efforts of team mates or opponents regardless of gender, ethnic origin or special needs. Always thank your opponents and officials after the game or fixture. Sport Codes Badminton Basketball Football Hockey Netball Rugby Softball Volleyball Touch Tag Athletics Weighlifting Student Athlete Policy Attendance and Attitude Requirements for Participating in Co-curricular Activities A condition for participating in co-curricular activities for the college is the student will have the correct attitude towards their schooling and at the same time will have acceptable attendance. If either of these is not the case, students will be withdrawn from their activity as set out below. Attendance If there is a serious concern about a student’s attendance they will be withdrawn from the activity. This would happen after there have been discussions with parents by the HOH about attendance concerns and the poor attendance continues. This includes period by period absences or non attendance at school. Step 1: Withdrawn from the activity for 1 week Step 2: After further interventions the attendance continues to be an issue, the student is withdrawn from the activity for 2 weeks. Step 3: After further interventions the attendance continues to be an issue, the student is withdrawn from the activity for the rest of the ‘season’ Discipline Issues / Poor Attitudes‍ Students who have been stood down: 1st stand down – the student is withdrawn from the activity while on stand down 2nd or 3rd stand down – the student will be withdrawn from the activity while on stand-down and while she/he is on red report. If the student is referred to SMT by HOH for behavioural issues, the student will be withdrawn from the activity while she/he is on red report. Suspension: If a student returns to school, the BOT imposes conditions on the student. Individual circumstance may be taken into consideration. ‍ ‍ ‍ Fees and Registration A fee is charged for all codes, covering essentials such as registration/entry and affiliation fees. There may be some additional costs throughout the year. We kindly ask that these fees are paid, or an automatic payment is arranged before the first game to ensure everything runs smoothly. Payment Options1. Internet Banking: ASB 12-3031-0837472-00 Reference: “Student Name” Particulars: “ID Number “. 2. Pay by cash, eftpos, or cheque (Payable to Rosehill College) at the cashier Office. Their hours are 8am – 3pm each day.
Sports

Our college is committed to fostering a culture of sportsmanship and integrity, encouraging active participation in a diverse range of sports. We believe in the importance of hard work and honesty, both on and off the field.

Our dedicated staff members are passionate about nurturing the holistic development of our students, recognising that sports play a crucial role in creating enriching experiences. Here, hard work and honesty are not just values we uphold—they are achievements we celebrate.

External Information

For more information regarding Rosehill College sports, please visit our external sports information page. Here, you will find details on fixtures, results, team rosters, training schedules, and other relevant sports-related matters. Rosehill College Sports Page
Follow Us
Rosehill College Sports Contact
Sports DirectorSaua Leaupepetele s.leaupepetele@rosehillcollege.school.nz
Sports Coordinator sports@rosehillcollege.school.nz
Sports Office Phone  09 295 0661 Ext 935
Code of Conduct
All students participating in Sport Department activities must:
Be reliable by attending all meetings, practices and games.
Wear the correct uniform, where the uniform is borrowed from the sports office it is to be maintained in a neat and tidy condition and returned at the end of the playing season.
Abide by the “Student Athlete Policy” of Rosehill College – includes attendance, class performance and behaviour.
Fair Play Rules
Treat opponents with respect.
Play hard but within the rules.
Exercise self control at all time (towards opponents, officials and spectators).
Respect and accept the decisions of officials without gesture or argument.
Always do your best but accept victory or defeat with dignity.
Show that it is a privilege to represent your school.
Recognise and applaud honestly the efforts of team mates or opponents regardless of gender, ethnic origin or special needs.
Always thank your opponents and officials after the game or fixture.
Sport Codes Badminton Basketball Football Hockey Netball Rugby Softball Volleyball Touch Tag Athletics Weighlifting
Sport Codes
Student Athlete Policy
Attendance and Attitude Requirements for Participating in Co-curricular Activities

A condition for participating in co-curricular activities for the college is the student will have the correct attitude towards their schooling and at the same time will have acceptable attendance. If either of these is not the case, students will be withdrawn from their activity as set out below.
Attendance

If there is a serious concern about a student’s attendance they will be withdrawn from the activity. This would happen after there have been discussions with parents by the HOH about attendance concerns and the poor attendance continues. This includes period by period absences or non attendance at school.
Step 1: Withdrawn from the activity for 1 week
Step 2: After further interventions the attendance continues to be an issue, the student is withdrawn from the activity for 2 weeks.
Step 3: After further interventions the attendance continues to be an issue, the student is withdrawn from the activity for the rest of the ‘season’
Discipline Issues / Poor Attitudes‍
Students who have been stood down:
1st stand down – the student is withdrawn from the activity while on stand down
2nd or 3rd stand down – the student will be withdrawn from the activity while on stand-down and while she/he is on red report.
If the student is referred to SMT by HOH for behavioural issues, the student will be withdrawn from the activity while she/he is on red report.
Suspension: If a student returns to school, the BOT imposes conditions on the student.
Individual circumstance may be taken into consideration.
Fees and Registration

A fee is charged for all codes, covering essentials such as registration/entry and affiliation fees. There may be some additional costs throughout the year. We kindly ask that these fees are paid, or an automatic payment is arranged before the first game to ensure everything runs smoothly.
Payment Options1. Internet Banking: ASB 12-3031-0837472-00 Reference: “Student Name” Particulars: “ID Number “.
2. Pay by cash, eftpos, or cheque (Payable to Rosehill College) at the cashier Office. Their hours are 8am – 3pm each day.
